already_implemented.server_listing: Lists all MCP servers from sync.data.mcp via <List> component with search/filter
already_implemented.toggle_connect: Switch toggles connect/disconnect via sdk.client.mcp.connect({name}) / disconnect({name})
already_implemented.add_remote_server: Add form with name + URL fields, creates McpRemoteConfig { type: 'remote', url, enabled: true } via sdk.client.mcp.add({name, config})
already_implemented.delete_button: Dropdown menu with 'Delete' item — BUT only calls disconnectMutation (just disconnects, doesn't remove)
already_implemented.auth_trigger: Clicking a needs_auth server triggers sdk.client.mcp.auth.authenticate({name})
already_implemented.status_labels: Connected/Disabled/Failed/Needs auth/Needs client registration i18n labels
already_implemented.mode_switching: List mode ↔ Add form mode with back-arrow navigation
already_implemented.form_validation: Empty name/URL checks with error display

missing_for_full_CRUD.proper_delete: handleRemove only calls sdk.client.mcp.disconnect — does NOT call sdk.client.mcp.remove (which doesn't exist in the API yet). Server entry persists in list after 'delete'
missing_for_full_CRUD.edit_existing_server: No edit mode, no dialog-edit-mcp component, no sdk.client.mcp.update (doesn't exist)
missing_for_full_CRUD.local_command_servers: Add form hardcodes McpRemoteConfig { type: 'remote' }. Cannot add local/stdio MCP servers (type: 'local' with command[])
missing_for_full_CRUD.environment_variables: No UI for editing environment variables (local servers) or headers (remote servers)
missing_for_full_CRUD.oauth_config: No OAuth config UI (clientId, clientSecret, scope, callbackPort)
missing_for_full_CRUD.enable_disable: No lightweight enable/disable — switch triggers full connect/disconnect cycle
missing_for_full_CRUD.confirmation_dialogs: No 'Are you sure?' confirmation before delete/disconnect

2_mcp_ipc_inventory.architecture: NO MCP-specific Electron IPC channels exist. The desktop app communicates with the MCP service exclusively through REST API calls via the SDK client to the sidecar server (HTTP).

relevant_generic_methods: storeGet(name, key) — generic key-value store read
storeSet(name, key, value) — generic key-value store write
storeDelete(name, key) — generic key-value store delete
storeKeys(name) — list all keys in a store

actual_data_flow.description: Renderer → SDK client (HTTP) → Sidecar (opencode server) → MCP Service

local_server.timeout: number? — ms, default: 5000 (service default) or 30000 (connection default)

remote_server.url: string — required. URL of the MCP server
remote_server.oauth: McpOAuthConfig | false? — OAuth config or explicit disable

oauth_config.clientId: string? — If omitted, dynamic client registration (RFC 7591) is attempted

persistence.config_file: ~/.opencode/data/opencode.json — the `mcp` field is a Record<string, ConfigMCP.Info>
persistence.dynamic_adds_ephemeral: CRITICAL: sdk.client.mcp.add() only stores in-memory (s.config[name] = mcp) — does NOT write to config file. Servers added dynamically are lost on restart!
persistence.static_config: Servers defined in opencode.json/opencode.yaml config file persist across restarts

4_tui_mcp_subcommand_map.finding: NO DEDICATED TUI MCP SUBCOMMANDS EXIST
4_tui_mcp_subcommand_map.details: Search for 'mcp' in packages/opencode/src/cli/ found zero results. MCP management is fully delegated to: 1) HTTP API (dynamic) 2) Config file (static) 3) Desktop dialog UI

what_each_command_would_do.list/status: GET /mcp → sdk.client.mcp.status() — already implemented in dialog
what_each_command_would_do.add: POST /mcp → sdk.client.mcp.add({name, config: ConfigMCP.Info}) — partially implemented (remote only)
what_each_command_would_do.edit/update: NO API METHOD EXISTS — would need MCP.Service.add() replacement or new update() method
what_each_command_would_do.remove/delete: NO API METHOD EXISTS — MCP.Service has add(), connect(), disconnect() but no remove(). Dynamic removes would need config file write
what_each_command_would_do.auth: POST /mcp/:name/auth → sdk.client.mcp.auth.start/authenticate — partially implemented via toggle click on needs_auth servers
what_each_command_would_do.logout: DELETE /mcp/:name/auth → sdk.client.mcp.auth.remove — not implemented in UI

5_recommended_implementation_boundary.phase_2_scope: MCP Server Management UI — Full CRUD
5_recommended_implementation_boundary.consumer_purpose: Desktop users can add, edit, remove, configure, and authenticate MCP servers (both local and remote) with proper persistence
claim_atoms: Dialog supports adding both local (command-based) and remote (URL-based) MCP servers
Dialog supports editing existing server configuration (name, URL/command, environment, headers, timeout, OAuth)
Dialog supports removing MCP servers with confirmation and persistence
Dialog supports OAuth authentication flow (login/logout) for remote servers
MCP server list shows health status, enables/disables, and supports search/filter
MCP config changes persist across app restarts (written to config file or electron-store)
Follows the same UI pattern as dialog-manage-agents.tsx (List with search, create/edit/delete actions)

key_design_decisions.persistence_strategy: Currently MCP.Service.add() only stores in-memory. Two options: A) Add remove()/update() to MCP.Service + write config file. B) Store MCP configs in electron-store (like custom agents) and inject into MCP.Service on startup. Option B is simpler but creates a parallel config source.
key_design_decisions.sdk_method_gap: The SDK client has no remove() or update() methods for MCP servers. These need to be added to both MCP.Service and the HTTP API handlers.
key_design_decisions.local_vs_remote_form: The add form needs a type selector (local/remote) that toggles between command[] fields and URL field
key_design_decisions.edit_dialog_pattern: dialog-edit-agent.tsx is the reference pattern: props-based inline dialog with form fields and onSave callback

dependencies: NO changes to packages/opencode if MCP config is stored via electron-store (Option B)
NO changes to packages/desktop IPC — use SDK client directly
MAY need to add sdk.client.mcp.remove() and/or sdk.client.mcp.update() to the HTTP API
MAY need to add remove()/update() to MCP.Service for config file persistence

primary.reason: Main MCP management dialog — already has 261 lines of WIP changes. Needs: proper delete, edit, local server support, OAuth UI
NEW FILE — edit MCP server dialog (follow dialog-edit-agent.tsx pattern). Form fields for name, type, command/url, environment, headers, timeout, OAuth config
NEW FILE — shared MCP config types for the UI layer (McpFormData, MCP server config fields)

secondary.reason: MAY need to add MCP config storage IPC handlers (if using electron-store for persistence, like custom agents)
MAY need to add MCP config ElectronAPI methods (if using electron-store)
MAY need to bridge MCP config IPC channels

backend_if_needed.reason: IF adding remove/update to MCP.Service. Add remove(name) and update(name, config) methods to Interface and implementation
IF adding remove/update OpenAPI route definitions

cross_cutting_findings.finding: MCP.Service.add() is ephemeral — config stored only in memory, lost on restart
No remove() or update() methods on MCP.Service interface
dialog-select-mcp.tsx 'Delete' button only disconnects, doesn't remove server from list
No electron-store namespace reserved for MCP (only 'desktop-custom-agents')
dialog-select-mcp.tsx has 261 uncommitted lines — in-progress Phase 1 work that needs careful preservation
No test coverage for MCP UI components
